OpenSFS Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(7 inline comments)

the procedure to convert fldb from index to a flat file should be done another
way, IMHO. first of all, I'd suggest do not iterate twice: to declare, then to
to update. just do one iteration and fill the cache with newly-generated offsets.
then another iteration other the cache to write to the flat file ?
before we even start convertion the target flat file should be truncated.
also, I think before proceeding after the convertion we need to destroy old index
and wait till everything is committed to a disk.

another aspect of the convertion is backward compatibility? having the convertion
and compatibility problems in mind, probably you're right about staying with index :)

Patch Set 4: (2 inline comments)

If the flat exists, it means the conversion has been done. then, why would we want truncate the old index file? 

Hmm, I thought about downgrade a bit, but no good idea. Andreas, any suggestion?  Again, even though the patches are here, I still thought we should keep index file to save us some trouble.
Uploaded patch set 5.

Patch Set 14:

forgot to ask.. is there a patch to set incompat flag so that once additional MDS is used 2.3 should not be able to use this filesystem? or we decided do not set it?

Patch Set 14:

Oh, because we do not change the fld format, even though there are entries which might point to other MDT, I do not see why 2.3 can not use this "new" fldb after downgrade.  So I did not add incompat flag here.

Patch Set 14:

well, this is not about fldb format.. this is rather about the fact that 2.3 won't be able to use filesystem with two MDSs. though this can be done later.

Patch Set 18:

Just found that we actually use local seq for root fid [0x200000001:0x6:0x0]  on ZFS which fld_index_lookup can not handle.  (It works in current master because we do not do fld lookup for zfs)

So we will either add local index seq entry into fldb or we need land "special root fid" patch first.

Patch Set 18: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

mark it - before root fid problem was fixed for ZFS.

Patch Set 18:

Di, can this patch and the next one be rebased on top of Mike's special root fid patch?  If there is anything you can do to speed up landing that patch (inspection, triage failure, restart testing, ping Oleg, etc) that would also help.

I'm going to marginally give this a pass, since I definitely want it to land.  While the client-side hack is mostly harmless, it does imply that old clients would be broken trying to access ZFS filesystems?

Is there a patch underway to add the special root FID handling for ZFS?  If yes, please ping me on it so that it can be inspected more quickly.  If not, please file a bug and make it a 2.4 blocker so that this is fixed properly.

parents.subject: LU-2378 lma: move HSM & SOM attributes to dedicated xattrs

commit.subject: LU-2240 fld: Move range lookup to fld.
commit.message:
LU-2240 fld: Move range lookup to fld.

Because ZFS does not support range lookup in a ZAP, we will move
range lookup  to FLD, and do an in-memory lookup there.  The FLD
entries are still stored in an index on disk, but this is just a
container and is not used for searching anymore.

It is expected the FLD will always be able to fit in RAM.

Signed-off-by: Wang Di <di.wang@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: If8cadcc30743e6d63ba779a430bc585af29d51fe
